title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants: (1) restore UID-based owner matching in `secretToKeystoneMapper` or at least accept any version in the `keystone.openstack.c5c3.io` group to survive API version bumps, (2) optionally verify referenced Keystone CRs exist before enqueueing to avoid queuing on stale ownerRefs, and (3) capture the `keystonePaths()` error in `envtest_setup.go:267` instead of discarding it with `_`, plus a minor "API-server" → "API server" typo fix in the docs.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- The updated owner-ref path in secretToKeystoneMapper now matches only on Kind and exact APIVersion, dropping the previous UID-based isOwnedBy check; consider either retaining a UID-based match or at least accepting any version in the keystone.openstack.c5c3.io group so existing Secrets continue to map correctly across version bumps.
- secretToKeystoneMapper currently enqueues reconcile requests purely from ownerReferences without verifying that the referenced Keystone objects actually exist; if spurious or stale ownerRefs are a concern, you may want to guard this with an existence check or a lightweight cache lookup to avoid queuing work for non-existent CRs.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The updated owner-ref path in secretToKeystoneMapper now matches only on Kind and exact APIVersion, dropping the previous UID-based isOwnedBy check; consider either retaining a UID-based match or at least accepting any version in the keystone.openstack.c5c3.io group so existing Secrets continue to map correctly across version bumps.
- secretToKeystoneMapper currently enqueues reconcile requests purely from ownerReferences without verifying that the referenced Keystone objects actually exist; if spurious or stale ownerRefs are a concern, you may want to guard this with an existence check or a lightweight cache lookup to avoid queuing work for non-existent CRs.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go" line_range="267" />
<code_context>
+) (ctrl.Manager, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	crdDir, _ := keystonePaths()
+
+	env := &envtest.Environment{
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (testing):** Silently discarding keystonePaths error makes envtest startup failures harder to diagnose.

The blank identifier ignores errors from `keystonePaths()`. If it fails (for example, when the CRD directory is missing), envtest will later error in a less obvious way. Please capture and handle the error explicitly—like the other helpers do—so tests fail fast with a clear message, e.g. `crdDir, err := keystonePaths(); if err != nil { t.Fatalf("...: %v", err) }`.
</issue_to_address>

### Comment 2
<location path="docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md" line_range="91-93" />
<code_context>
+The Keystone controller registers a controller-runtime field indexer on the
+`Keystone` kind so that a Secret event is resolved to the referencing Keystone
+CR(s) via an O(1) cache lookup instead of an unfiltered namespace-scoped List.
+Without the indexer, every Secret create/update/delete event in a namespace
+containing ESO-managed Secrets would force the mapper to List every Keystone CR
+in that namespace — producing API-server load that scales linearly with the
+number of Secret events, not with the number of Keystone CRs (CC-0087, REQ-001).
+
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**nitpick (typo):** Consider changing "API-server" to "API server" for consistency with common Kubernetes terminology.

Here, "producing API-server load" could be updated to "producing API server load" to better match Kubernetes documentation conventions.

Suggested implementation:

```
Without the indexer, every Secret create/update/delete event in a namespace
containing ESO-managed Secrets would force the mapper to List every Keystone CR
in that namespace — producing API server load that scales linearly with the
number of Secret events, not with the number of Keystone CRs (CC-0087, REQ-001).

```

You may want to run a project-wide search for "API-server" and similarly update any other occurrences to "API server" for consistency across the documentation.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

code_comments.body:
**issue (testing):** Silently discarding keystonePaths error makes envtest startup failures harder to diagnose.

The blank identifier ignores errors from `keystonePaths()`. If it fails (for example, when the CRD directory is missing), envtest will later error in a less obvious way. Please capture and handle the error explicitly—like the other helpers do—so tests fail fast with a clear message, e.g. `crdDir, err := keystonePaths(); if err != nil { t.Fatalf("...: %v", err) }`.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Not changed; an inline REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment was added at envtest_setup.go:267 explaining that keystonePaths() returns (crdDir, webhookDir string) — the blank identifier discards the unused webhook directory rather than an error, and ErrorIfCRDPathMissing=true already fails fast on a missing CRD path.

code_comments.body:
**nitpick (typo):** Consider changing "API-server" to "API server" for consistency with common Kubernetes terminology.

Here, "producing API-server load" could be updated to "producing API server load" to better match Kubernetes documentation conventions.

Suggested implementation:

```
Without the indexer, every Secret create/update/delete event in a namespace
containing ESO-managed Secrets would force the mapper to List every Keystone CR
in that namespace — producing API server load that scales linearly with the
number of Secret events, not with the number of Keystone CRs (CC-0087, REQ-001).

```

You may want to run a project-wide search for "API-server" and similarly update any other occurrences to "API server" for consistency across the documentation.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Addressed: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:93 changed 'API-server load' to 'API server load'; same fix also applied to a matching 'API-server amplification' comment in keystone_controller_test.go for consistency.

resolution_summary: Both high-level review concerns were addressed directly in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:secretToKeystoneMapper: (1) owner-ref matching was loosened from exact APIVersion equality to group-only matching via schema.ParseGroupVersion, so existing Secrets survive an API version bump; (2) a cached client.Get against the informer cache was added to drop owner-refs whose target Keystone is NotFound, while non-NotFound errors fall through to enqueue so a transient cache blip cannot swallow a legitimate event. Beyond the two code-comment locations, corresponding changes were also made to: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md (rewrote the Owner-ref fallback row to describe group-only matching + the existence-check behavior — necessary to keep the reference doc consistent with the new code), and operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go (added five new unit tests covering same-group/different-version match, foreign-group ignore, malformed APIVersion, stale NotFound skip, and transient Get-error enqueue — necessary to lock in the new behavior; also fixed one 'API-server' comment typo for consistency with the doc nit). Comment [0] was rejected with an inline REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT note because keystonePaths() returns (crdDir, webhookDir string) with no error — the blank identifier discards the webhook path, not an error.
